key_insight: A coupled thermal-mechanical ABAQUS model shows that 6082 aluminum butt welds develop peak temperatures in the weld zone, with residual stress and deformation concentrated in the weld seam and heat-affected zone.
engineering_relevance: The results help predict welding-induced distortion and stress, supporting better process settings and structural design for aluminum alloy welded joints.
method: Built a 3D temperature-displacement coupling model in ABAQUS for 6082 aluminum butt welding.
Performed transient thermal analysis to compute the welding temperature field.
Used the temperature history in a mechanical analysis to estimate residual stress and deformation.
evidence_signal: The study evaluates simulated temperature distribution, residual stress concentration, and deformation behavior during the welding thermal cycle.
watch_item: Because the conclusion is based on numerical simulation, accuracy depends on material parameters, boundary conditions, and validation against experiments.
paper_title: Thermal-Mechanical Analysis of Residual Stress and Deformation in 6082 Aluminum Alloy Welded Joints
